note: M3 — ViewSpec contract (design contract: docs/novakai/m3-viewspec-design.md). One serializable spec object becomes the reading view: screen = render(spec). Map-first authoring: the fragment nodes land in the bundle BEFORE the code, so every change is a modify against the current map (the add->modify lifecycle flip that bit three prior sessions is avoided by construction); the falsifiable half is the acceptance cases below — red until src/core/viewspec/viewspec.ts implements them, green only when behaviour matches. First plan in this repo to carry H1 projection acceptance cases. Resume: npm run novakai:status -- --plan docs/novakai/plans/m3-viewspec.plan.json · prove behaviour: npm run novakai:acceptance -- --plan docs/novakai/plans/m3-viewspec.plan.json

intent.problem: The reading view is ~10 unserializable closure variables inside initUnfold; nothing outside unfold can read, write, validate or replay what is on screen — an LLM or the M9 ViewSpec editor has no object to write.
intent.approach: New pure core module (the core/plan pattern: types + pure helpers, no DOM, zero imports) holding the ViewSpec contract: the spec type, the action vocabulary, the schema boundary and the reducer.
intent.rationale: Keeping type + normalizer + reducer in one module makes the contract one reviewable map node; core/types stays the editor-model vocabulary.

intent.problem: No named, versioned shape exists for 'what the reading surface shows'; the prototype's spec object never made it into src.
intent.approach: interface ViewSpec: v:1, expanded, hidden, layers, sel, selWire, query, stage, focusType, fmOpen — plain JSON values only (Sets banned: a future LLM writes this object). Z (pan/zoom) is OUT by human verdict; animation infra and the derived model are out by the WHAT/HOW boundary (design doc section 2).

fm.name: ViewSpec
fm.description: serializable reading-view contract: screen = render(spec); checkboxes and a future LLM write the same object
state: selWire: { a: string; b: string } | null

intent.problem: persistView trusts localStorage JSON with a bare cast; garbage or a stale id set silently corrupts the session — and there is no schema anywhere ('schema validated' is the M3 manual note this replaces).
intent.approach: normalizeViewSpec(raw, known): tolerant field-by-field coercion of unknown JSON (full v1 spec, the legacy expanded/hidden/layers subset, or garbage) into a valid ViewSpec; the known-id list confines expanded/hidden/sel/selWire/stage to a real model; idempotent on a valid spec.
intent.rationale: known is a string[] (not a callback) so acceptance cases can carry it as JSON — the id-filtering half of the schema stays machine-testable.

fm.name: normalizeViewSpec
fm.description: tolerant schema boundary: coerce unknown JSON into a valid ViewSpec; a known-id list confines the spec to a real model; idempotent on a valid spec

cases.name: garbage yields the empty spec (calls layer ON — wires are the story)
legacy localStorage shape migrates branch-free; stored layer prefs win (calls not re-defaulted)
known-id list confines the spec: stale ids drop, wire nulls on one unknown endpoint, query survives

intent.problem: The view-mutation invariants (collapse folds descendants, reveal unhides the chain, last-root hide guard, the sel/selWire/focusType/fmOpen exclusions, stage invalidates selWire) live scattered across ~10 unfold handlers — unreviewable and untestable ('render purity confirmed in review' is the other half of the M3 manual note this replaces).
intent.approach: reduceView(spec, action, model): pure reducer over the closed ViewAction vocabulary; returns a NEW spec, never mutates its (possibly frozen) input; consults a plain-data ViewModelIndex so cases carry the containment as JSON.
intent.rationale: The reducer is where render(spec) becomes machine-provable instead of convention: each invariant is an acceptance case run against the real export.

fm.name: reduceView
fm.description: pure view reducer: new spec out, input never mutated; centralizes the scattered handler invariants

cases.name: collapse folds ALL descendants, not just the target
reveal unhides the ancestor chain and expands the ancestors (not the node itself)
selectWire clears the mutually-exclusive focuses (sel, focusType, fmOpen)
hide refuses the last visible root (the canvas can never go empty)

intent.problem: Handlers mutate 10 closure variables directly then call hand-picked render subsets — 'no direct DOM-toggle handlers' has no boundary a rule (or an LLM) can hold onto.
intent.approach: Single mutation entry commit(a) = reduceView -> deep-freeze install -> paint(a). paint transcribes today's per-action render subsets (the approved animation quality) as an internal optimization BEHIND the boundary; the old closure view variables are deleted, so shadow state is a compile error and a stray direct write throws on the frozen spec.
intent.rationale: Machine form of the rule: the roadmap's negative-grep predicate proves the old variables are gone; the gate proves the commit node's shape. An inner closure is not importable, so this change carries no acceptance cases — its invariants are proven at the reducer.
intent.tradeoff: One behavioural move that is not a pure transcription: the selWire dies-with-its-reps rule leaves render() (a render-time state write) and moves into the reducer/normalizer path.

fm.name: commit
fm.description: the ONLY view-mutation entry: reduceView -> frozen install -> per-action paint

intent.problem: persistView serializes an ad-hoc {expanded,hidden,layers} triple — a second, divergent serialization of view state with an unvalidated load path.
intent.approach: Store the full v1 spec; load through normalizeViewSpec(raw, [...U.keys()]) and apply the durable trio (expanded/hidden/layers, trust gated on TRUST_SRC at the call site). Key 'unfold.view', sorted-roots fingerprint and the 24-entry cap are unchanged; a pre-M3 stored entry is a valid subset, so migration is branch-free.
intent.tradeoff: sel/stage/query are stored (the format carries them) but deliberately not restored — selectSync owns selection at the mode boundary; restoring them is an M4 decision.

fm.name: persistView
fm.description: reading session persists per diagram as the full v1 ViewSpec; load goes through the schema boundary and applies the durable trio
